performance_overview: Through 51 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull holds 6 open positions after META stopped out at today's open. Account equity ~$99,163 (-0.84% since $100K inception) vs SPY -2.93% cumulative — Bull's lead over the benchmark WIDENED to ~+2.09pts (from +1.13 yesterday) as the defensive book massively outperformed a broad selloff. Realized record moves to 11 round-trips, 3W/8L (the trend book skews to small winners held vs a handful of full 2xATR stop-losses). Today (Jul 23 EOD) was a decisive risk-off day: SPY -1.51%, yet Bull lost only -$617.82 (-0.62%) for +0.89% alpha — the defensive tilt did exactly its job. The headline event: META's GTC 2xATR hard stop ($613.58) filled automatically at the 9:33 AM ET open (11 sh @ $608.06, GAPPING DOWN ~$5.52 through the trigger), realizing -$584.45 (-8.04%) on an 8-day hold. This confirms the prior EOD/morning call that flagged META as the #1 stop-out watch — the extended-entry (~8.5% above EMA-20) digestion range broke lower and the position bled straight into its stop. NOTE: the morning agent took the XLE entry ~1 min after META's fill but did not reconcile the stop-out, so this EOD agent logged the META EXIT and removed it from positions.json (recurring stop self-fill unlogged bug, now 8th occurrence, 2nd consecutive day after CMG). Surviving book is net-positive and mostly green: BMY new book leader +4.79% (~10.3% above stop), SCHW +3.67%, PCG +2.17%, XLU firmed to +1.18%. Two mild reds: GIS -0.52% (now the tightest-to-stop at +3.76%) and today's fresh XLE entry -1.22% (Day 0, thin-cross reversal watch).
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the ONLY consistently profitable bucket for the LEGACY intraday RSI strategy; n=2, thin sample, informational only now that the strategy is MA-20/60 trend-following. Do not act on it without n>=10.

Shallow-cross probation entries (heavy size-DOWN, not skip) continue to validate under trend-following: BMY (0.13% cross, 50% unit) is the current book leader at +4.79% on Day 7; GIS (0.29% cross, 75% unit) held +2.71% through Day 8 before easing to -0.52% today. Sustained read that a size-down (NOT a skip) is the right call for shallow-but-non-earnings-adjacent golden crosses above the 0.1% floor. Still only 2-3 samples; keep sizing down, do not upsize. XLE (0.14% cross, 50% unit, entered today) is the newest test of this rule.
avoid: Extended entries (price well above EMA-20 at entry) — NOW A CONFIRMED LOSER: META entered Jul 15 at ~8.5% above EMA-20, peaked +2.83%, chopped in a week-long digestion range, then broke LOWER and stopped out at -8.04% (-$584.45) at today's open. The full arc played out exactly as the early caution predicted: chasing an extended golden cross bought the pullback and then bled into the 2xATR stop. Firm rule: treat large above-EMA extension (>~6-8%) as a size-DOWN or wait-for-pullback. n=1 realized but a clean, complete cautionary case.

Shallow EMA cross entries on earnings-adjacent names: TSLA (Jul 7 entry, 0.06% cross spread, earnings Jul 22) lost -4.78% on exit. Rule: SKIP (do not size down) when BOTH shallow cross spread (<0.5%) AND earnings within 14 days apply simultaneously. Live rule under the current trend strategy.

Very shallow golden crosses (<0.1% EMA-20/60 spread): NVDA (0.07%) skipped Jul 15 as a near-clone of the failed TSLA pattern (0.06%). Treat sub-0.1% crosses as noise-level, high reversal risk — skip on sight. BMY at 0.13% cleared this line by a hair, taken at half unit, and is now the book leader (+4.79%), validating 0.1% as the skip floor.

Earnings-blackout holds ride to the hard stop with no discretionary exit: CMG (entered Jul 10, earnings blackout Jul 15-29) drifted to its 2xATR stop and filled automatically at -8.06% on Jul 22. The blackout policy working as intended — no death-cross override during blackout means the full 2xATR is the risk. Record of realized blackout downside, not a rule change.

LEGACY intraday RSI buckets (informational, strategy retired): RSI 60-65 0/3, RSI 65-75 0/1, gap-up opens with RSI>60 — all losers historically. Retained only as a record of why the intraday RSI approach was abandoned in favor of trend-following.
recent_market_context: Jul 17-23: MA-20/60 trend strategy running; book at 6 positions after two consecutive automatic stop-outs (CMG Jul 22, META Jul 23). Broad market has been choppy-to-down and is now clearly risk-off: after a decisive risk-on rally Jul 21 (SPY +0.85%) where Bull's defensive tilt lagged (-0.92% alpha), the tape rolled over — flat Jul 22 (SPY -0.05%, Bull +0.07% alpha) then a decisive down Jul 23 (SPY -1.51%, Bull only -0.62% for +0.89% alpha). Bull's defensive book is winning the down days and lagging the up days, as designed. Only 1 new golden cross has cleared filters recently (XLE, taken today); scan candidate flow is thin. VIX last explicit read 16.64 (Jul 23 morning); given today's -1.51% SPY selloff it has very likely ticked higher — evening scan should refresh it. Trending/choppy: choppy with a downside tilt developing over the last two sessions.

current_open_positions.notes: Day 17; stop $93.28 (fixed, 2xATR). Green +3.67%, ~8.8% above stop. Full unit, no death cross. Anchor position behaving normally; firmed on the down day.
Day 17; stop $16.29. Eased from +5.50% to +2.17%, ~7.4% above stop. Still the steadiest low-beta uptrend in the book. Full unit, no death cross.
Day 17; stop $44.37. Utilities held up on the risk-off day — firmed to +1.18%, ~4.1% above GTC $44.37. Oldest position, weak but positive trend. No death cross. Full unit.

Day 9; stop $34.41. Slipped to -0.52% and is now the TIGHTEST-TO-STOP at +3.76% above GTC $34.41 (overtaking XLU). 75% unit (207 of 276) shallow-cross probation. New #1 stop-out watch — monitor for a death cross tonight.

Day 7; stop $55.76 (2xATR=1.4723). NEW BOOK LEADER +4.79%, ~10.3% above stop. 50% unit (85 of 171 full), heaviest size-down on the shallowest cross (0.13% EMA20/60). Shallow-cross size-down strongly validating. No death cross.

Day 0 (entered today at open @ $60.17). Golden cross, shallow 0.14% EMA20/60 -> HALF unit (84 of 168) per shallow-cross playbook, same bucket as BMY. Already -1.22% (energy weak on the risk-off tape), ~4.4% above stop $56.92 (fill - 2xATR 1.6239). Secondary reversal watch given thin cross + Day-0 red. Keep half size, do not upsize.

INFRA (recurring, escalate to routine owner): (a) Alpaca returned acct.last_equity=0 today, which made update_pnl.py compute a garbage daily P&L (starting_equity=0 -> pnl=$99k); I hand-corrected daily_pnl.json using yesterday's close ($99,781.17) as starting_equity — update_pnl.py needs a guard against last_equity==0 (fall back to prior file's current_equity). (b) The stop self-fill reconciliation gap has now hit 2 consecutive days (CMG, META) — the market-open routine should re-check for filled stop orders / vanished positions before/after taking new entries, not leave it to EOD. (c) Part-3/Part-4a routine snippets still double-nest data/data/ via lib.state _resolve when passed 'data/'-prefixed paths; use bare filenames.
